symptoms.symptom_name: Profuse watery diarrhoea (>10 stools/day) worsening over 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-volume watery diarrhoea is the cardinal feature of immune-mediated colitis and drives the volume loss.
Also causes acute watery diarrhoea — non-specific in isolation.
New severe diarrhoea 1-3 weeks after a checkpoint-inhibitor dose is the defining temporal signature of an immune-related adverse event; it reframes the whole presentation.
Does not exclude infection, but makes an immune aetiology more likely and mandates urgent oncology-aware assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pruritic maculopapular rash preceding the diarrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A concurrent cutaneous irAE points to systemic immune toxicity rather than a purely enteric infection.
A preceding immune-type rash is not a feature of ordinary infective gastroenteritis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: severe immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis with hypovolaemic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxy for circulatory compromise; converts a diarrhoea presentation into a shock emergency requiring 000.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: severe immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis with hypovolaemic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to replace losses orally means oral rehydration will fail — an independent indication for IV fluids in hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unusual food, no travel, no recent antibiotics

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No dietary/travel exposure lowers the prior for classic infective gastroenteritis.
No recent antibiotic or healthcare exposure lowers (but does not exclude) C. difficile.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: severe immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis with hypovolaemic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective distributive/hypovolaemic shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from the reported near-syncope and a wearable heart-rate proxy, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse abdominal tenderness with hyperactive bowel sounds, no rebound or rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with active colitis without peritonism.
Absence of rebound/rigidity argues against established perforation now — but does not exclude progression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor skin turgor, delayed capillary refill (4s), thready pulses, sunken eyes, dry mucosa

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: severe immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis with hypovolaemic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective severe-dehydration signs — clinician-only; corroborate the shock state sealed on the answer-key side.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloods (U&E/renal function, CRP, FBE), stool studies, and hospital endoscopy/CT

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urgent bloods (electrolytes/renal function/CRP), stool microbiology including C. difficile toxin, and in-hospital endoscopy/CT as indicated
investigation_details.expected_result: Electrolyte derangement and acute kidney injury from volume loss; inflammatory markers raised; stool studies to exclude infection; endoscopy showing inflammatory colitis if performed
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity/complications and infective mimics cannot be assessed — all require in-person/hospital pathways the AI cannot provide.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: severe immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis with hypovolaemic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grading of colitis, quantifying volume/renal impact and excluding infection are hospital investigations, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe diarrhoea + preceding skin rash + recent checkpoint-inhibitor dose together signal a systemic immune-related adverse event, not simple gastroenteritis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: severe immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis with hypovolaemic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Near-syncope, inability to tolerate fluids, tachycardia/hypotension and dehydration signs together define an emergency requiring 000 and IV resuscitation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent electrolytes/renal function, CRP and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify volume/renal impact of massive fluid loss and inflammatory burden — done in hospital after emergency transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool microbiology including Clostridioides difficile toxin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude an infective cause in parallel with treating the suspected immune colitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-hospital flexible sigmoidoscopy/colonoscopy or CT abdomen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm and grade colitis and detect complications (perforation/toxic megacolon) — specialist hospital investigation, not first-contact.
Anti-diarrhoeal agents are avoided in immune-mediated/severe colitis — they can precipitate toxic megacolon and mask deterioration.
